name_en: Alliance of Builders of Islamic Iran
description: The Alliance of Builders of Islamic Iran (Abadgaran) was a conservative-principlist political coalition formed in 2003. Described as 'Iran's neocons', it was composed mainly of [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) and [Basij](/orgs/basij) veterans who had been marginalised under [Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani) and [Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami). It swept the 2003 municipal and 2004 parliamentary elections — helped enormously by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council)'s mass disqualification of reformist candidates — and backed [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s successful 2005 presidential run. [Gholam-Ali Haddad-Adel](/people/gholam-ali-haddad-adel), the [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) Speaker from 2004–2008, was a key figure.
editorial_notes: Founded 2003. Won majority in 7th Majlis (2004) with Guardian Council assistance. Backed Ahmadinejad's 2005 presidential victory. Largely dissolved as a distinct entity after 2007.
